mission.title: Install and Configure ClickHouse
mission.description: ClickHouse is a real-time analytics database management system designed for generating analytical data reports quickly and efficiently. It is ideal for applications requiring high-speed data processing and real-time analytics. Key trade-offs include its column-oriented storage format, which optimizes read performance but may require more complex data modeling.

steps.title: Add ClickHouse Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official ClickHouse Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add clickhouse https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse

```

> This repository contains the ClickHouse Helm charts.

steps.title: Install ClickHouse CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for ClickHouse to function properly in Kubernetes.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/v26.2.4.23-stable/deploy/crds/clickhouse.altinity.com_clickhouses.yaml

```

> This CRD allows Kubernetes to manage ClickHouse instances.

steps.title: Install ClickHouse using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs ClickHouse in the specified namespace using the Helm chart.

```bash

helm install clickhouse clickhouse/clickhouse --namespace clickhouse --create-namespace --version 26.2.4.23

```

> This command deploys ClickHouse with the specified version.

steps.title: Create a ClickHouse Cluster Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Custom Resource (CR) to define your ClickHouse cluster configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: clickhouse.altinity.com/v1
kind: ClickHouseCluster
metadata:
  name: my-clickhouse
  namespace: clickhouse
spec:
  layout: replicated
  templates:
    podTemplate:
      spec:
        containers:
          - name: clickhouse
            image: clickhouse/clickhouse-server:latest
EOF

```

> This creates a simple ClickHouse cluster.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: clickhouse.altinity.com/v1
kind: ClickHouseCluster
metadata:
  name: my-clickhouse
  namespace: clickhouse
spec:
  layout: replicated
  templates:
    podTemplate:
      spec:
        containers:
          - name: clickhouse
            image: clickhouse/clickhouse-server:latest
EOF

steps.title: Verify ClickHouse Cluster Status
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the ClickHouse cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get clickhousecluster -n clickhouse

```

> You should see your ClickHouse cluster listed with its status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall ClickHouse
uninstall.description:
This step removes the ClickHouse deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss unless backups are taken.

```bash

helm uninstall clickhouse --namespace clickhouse

kubectl delete namespace clickhouse

```

> This command removes the ClickHouse release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade ClickHouse
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have backups of your data. This step will upgrade ClickHouse to the specified version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade clickhouse clickhouse/clickhouse --namespace clickhouse --version 26.2.4.23

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node you want to drain.
> This command upgrades ClickHouse to the specified version.
> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node you want to uncordon.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to ClickHouse server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The ClickHouse server may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the ClickHouse pod status with 'kubectl get pods -n clickhouse' and ensure it is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No nodes available for ClickHouse'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may not be enough resources available in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your Kubernetes cluster has sufficient resources allocated for ClickHouse.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ClickHouse cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The ClickHouse cluster may be in the process of starting up or has encountered an error.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the ClickHouse pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n clickhouse' for more information.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create ClickHouse database'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The ClickHouse user may not have sufficient permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the ClickHouse user has the necessary permissions to create databases.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new SQL functions and improved performance for large datasets.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Review the release notes for changes in SQL syntax and update your queries accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see the ClickHouse cluster listed with 'kubectl get clickhousecluster -n clickhouse' and its status should be 'Running'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
